title: Cohesion & Linking
subtitle: How ideas travel between sentences — without a traffic jam of 'however's
intro: Cohesion is how a paragraph hangs together. IELTS examiners reward variety and accuracy, not density: an essay packed with 'Moreover' and 'Firstly, Secondly, Thirdly' sounds memorised. Band 7+ writers use a balanced mix of sentence-initial connectors, mid-sentence conjunctions, and reference devices (pronouns, demonstratives, synonym chains) to thread ideas naturally.

branches.label: Lexical chains
children: Repeat key topic word in varied forms

rules.heading: Contrast vs. concession
rules.body: Contrast: however, on the other hand, whereas, while. Concession: although, even though, despite + noun/gerund, nevertheless, nonetheless. 'Despite the risks' (preposition) vs. 'Although there were risks' (conjunction).

rules.heading: Cause and result
rules.body: Cause: because / since / as + clause; due to / owing to + noun. Result: therefore, consequently, as a result, hence, thus. 'The data were inconclusive; consequently, the experiment was repeated.'

rules.heading: Addition and exemplification
rules.body: Addition: moreover, furthermore, in addition, additionally. Exemplification: for instance, for example, such as, namely, that is. Reserve 'Firstly/Secondly/Finally' for explicit enumeration — overuse looks mechanical.

rules.heading: Reference chains for cohesion
rules.body: Beyond connectors: use pronouns (this, these, such, it), demonstratives (this phenomenon, such measures), and lexical repetition with synonyms (the policy → the initiative → the reform) to tie sentences without adding connectors.

examples.sentence: Although public transport has expanded significantly, car ownership in most cities continues to rise.
The reform reduced waiting times. It also cut costs by fifteen per cent. Such outcomes are rare in large-scale healthcare projects.
Air pollution has intensified in recent decades; consequently, respiratory illness has become the leading cause of urban hospital admissions.
Despite repeated warnings from scientists, governments have been slow to act.
Renewable energy is now cost-competitive. Moreover, battery storage has advanced dramatically in the past five years.
examples.note: 'Moreover' adds information of the same type.

band6.text: Fast food is unhealthy. Also fast food causes obesity. Moreover, fast food is cheap. In conclusion fast food is bad.
band6.issue: Repetitive subject, bolt-on connectors ('Also', 'Moreover'), no true lexical chain.

band8.text: Although fast food is inexpensive, its nutritional profile is poor; consequently, widespread consumption has been linked to rising obesity rates.
band8.why: Subordination ('Although'), semicolon linkage, and a causal chain ('consequently…linked to') create tight cohesion without connector spam.

band8_marker: Band 8 writers use less visible cohesion. Instead of opening every sentence with a connector, they rely on thematic progression — one sentence's rheme (new info) becomes the next sentence's theme (starting point): 'The government introduced a carbon tax. The tax generated resistance from industry. Industry lobbying, in turn, led to a series of exemptions.' No explicit connector — the cohesion is built into the noun phrases.

common_errors.error: 'Despite of the warnings' / 'Although of the risks'
common_errors.fix: 'Despite' takes no 'of' → 'Despite the warnings'. 'Although' takes a clause → 'Although there were risks'.
Rotate connectors; use reference devices; let some sentences flow without an explicit connector.

common_errors.error: Comma-splice: 'Air quality is poor, therefore respiratory illness is common.'
common_errors.fix: Use a semi-colon or full stop → 'Air quality is poor; therefore, respiratory illness is common.'
Not standard English → 'In my view' / 'I would contend that…'

practice.title: Rewrite with the target connector
practice.instruction: Rewrite the sentence using the connector in brackets, changing structure as needed.

items.prompt: The factory was closed. It had been losing money for years. (as)
items.answer: The factory was closed as it had been losing money for years.

items.prompt: She studied hard. She did not pass the exam. (despite)
items.answer: Despite studying hard, she did not pass the exam.

items.prompt: Emissions are rising. The government has not introduced new taxes. (even though)
items.answer: Emissions are rising, even though the government has not introduced new taxes.

items.prompt: The team worked overtime. They finished the report on time. (consequently)
items.answer: The team worked overtime; consequently, they finished the report on time.

items.prompt: Public transport is cheap. Public transport is reliable. Many people use it. (because)
items.answer: Many people use public transport because it is cheap and reliable.

items.prompt: Electric vehicles are gaining popularity. Charging infrastructure remains limited. (whereas)
items.answer: Electric vehicles are gaining popularity, whereas charging infrastructure remains limited.

items.prompt: The policy was controversial. It was eventually adopted. (nevertheless)
items.answer: The policy was controversial; nevertheless, it was eventually adopted.

items.prompt: Heavy rain delayed construction. The project ran over budget. (as a result)
items.answer: Heavy rain delayed construction; as a result, the project ran over budget.

practice.title: Choose a fitting connector
practice.instruction: Fill in the blank with a single word or short phrase.

items.prompt: ___ the heavy rain, the match went ahead as scheduled.
The proposal was rejected ___ the committee felt it was too expensive.
The data were limited; ___ , the team decided to repeat the experiment.
___ his qualifications, he struggled to find a job.
Global trade has expanded dramatically; ___ , inequality between nations has widened.
Children who read regularly develop stronger vocabulary. ___ , they perform better in academic tests.
The law was passed quickly, ___ the opposition was severe.
___ the rise of streaming services, cinema attendance has declined.
items.answer: Due to / Because of / Owing to

items.prompt: Working from home has become common. ___ , it presents unique challenges for mental health.
The survey sample was small. ___ , the findings should be interpreted cautiously.
Young people are increasingly politically engaged, ___ previous generations at the same age.
Inflation has slowed ___ the central bank raised interest rates.
Air pollution damages lungs; ___ , it also impairs cognitive development.
The ancient manuscripts were damaged. ___ , conservators were able to restore most of them.
Several species are under threat, ___ the snow leopard and the Amur tiger.
Screen time has risen sharply. ___ , face-to-face interaction has declined by a third.
